findings.title: Public registration can create admin or teacher group members
findings.impact: An unauthenticated caller can obtain administrative or unapproved teacher privileges and reach protected student or operational data.
findings.trigger: POST /auth/register with role admin, teacher, or a normalized equivalent, followed by normal confirmation.
findings.evidence: The public route accepts an unrestricted role, selects a role client, writes custom:role, and adds the user to admins or teachers. A mocked local TestClient request with role=admin returned 201 and recorded admins group assignment.
findings.recommended_fix: Restrict public registration to approved public roles; provision admins through the audited admin workflow and teachers through authenticated, expiring, replay-safe invitations and approval.
required_tests: Reject privileged and unknown public roles

findings.title: Parent and teacher routes permit horizontal access to unrelated student data
findings.impact: Unrelated parents or teachers can read minors' learning records across family and assignment boundaries.
findings.trigger: Authenticate as a parent or teacher and request a known arbitrary student or question identifier.
findings.evidence: Affected routes check role but not parent binding or teacher assignment. A local unrelated-parent request to /students/arbitrary-child/summary returned 200.
findings.recommended_fix: Centralize resource authorization using active parent bindings, active teacher assignment/session, owner identity, and explicit admin permission, and apply it to every identifier-bearing route.

findings.title: Question OCR accepts another user's S3 object key
findings.impact: A student who obtains another upload key can extract and persist its OCR text, breaking user data isolation.
findings.trigger: Submit a question with image_s3_key owned by another user.
findings.evidence: Presign issuance creates an owner prefix, but question submission accepts any key and invokes Rekognition without checking that prefix or an upload ownership record.
findings.recommended_fix: Persist upload intent and owner, validate exact owner and object metadata after upload, and atomically consume the upload with question creation.

findings.title: Legacy practice responses expose correct answers before submission
findings.impact: Students can bypass exercises, invalidating mastery, adaptive learning, and outcome analytics.
findings.trigger: Request practice overview, path, or lesson before answering.
findings.evidence: _build_challenge always includes correctAnswer and every listed student response uses it.
findings.recommended_fix: Separate preview and result schemas and expose answer-derived fields only after a recorded answer or to an authorized author/admin view.
required_tests: Answer absent from all student previews
dependencies: Mobile and web curriculum consumer update

findings.title: Question quota, ledger, and question writes are non-atomic
findings.impact: Quota can be consumed without a question, ledger and question state can diverge, and retries can double count or become permanently conflicted.
findings.trigger: Any exception, timeout, or retry between counter increment, ledger put, and question put.
findings.evidence: The implementation explicitly records write_order=counter_then_ledger, followed by a separate question write; current tests also expose the ledger as an unaccounted side effect.
findings.recommended_fix: Use a DynamoDB transaction for conditional quota, idempotent ledger, and question state, or a recoverable outbox with explicit compensation and reconciliation.

findings.title: Stripe checkout creation can orphan or duplicate provider sessions
findings.impact: Ambiguous failures can produce orphan or duplicate checkout sessions and inconsistent billing UI/support state.
findings.trigger: DynamoDB failure or Lambda timeout after Stripe creates a session, followed by a retry.
findings.evidence: Provider creation precedes multiple independent local writes and Stripe Session.create receives no deterministic idempotency key. No live failure injection was performed.
findings.recommended_fix: Persist a billing command, use a deterministic Stripe idempotency key, transact local state, and reconcile ambiguous provider outcomes.
required_tests: Provider succeeds then local write fails

findings.title: Teacher takeover uses a read-then-write race
findings.impact: Two teachers can both receive success, create duplicate sessions and notifications, and leave last-writer-wins ownership.
findings.trigger: Concurrent takeover requests while a question is escalated.
findings.evidence: The route reads and validates state, performs an unconditional status update, then independently writes a session. Existing tests are sequential.
findings.recommended_fix: Conditionally update expected status/dispatch owner and atomically create the session; map the losing conditional write to 409.
dependencies: Conditional update and transaction repository support

findings.title: Every main push deploys production Lambda code without quality gates
findings.impact: Known-bad source can be deployed directly to both Lambda functions with no staging smoke or automatic rollback.
findings.trigger: Any push to main.
findings.evidence: The workflow builds then calls update-function-code for stoa-api and stoa-weekly-report; it has no pytest, lint, types, dependency scan, staging, approval, alias promotion, or rollback gate. Current main has failing tests.
findings.recommended_fix: Split verify/build/deploy, require protected checks, promote one immutable tested artifact through staging and Lambda aliases, and pin actions to immutable SHAs.

findings.title: Mobile app is a non-buildable placeholder skeleton
findings.impact: There is no reproducible mobile build or usable student/parent journey despite milestone claims.
findings.trigger: Clean dependency install, typecheck, native build, or opening most app routes.
findings.evidence: Most routes render StateCard placeholders; sign-in renders text only. No lockfile exists. An isolated npm install fails ETARGET for expo-constants@~19.0.0 and the configured notification icon is absent.
findings.recommended_fix: Choose a supported Expo SDK dependency matrix, commit a lockfile, make clean typecheck/native builds pass, and implement the core screens against verified contracts.

findings.title: Mobile Cognito flow bypasses backend identity provisioning and reads incompatible roles
findings.impact: Mobile registration can create orphan Cognito identities and restored sessions can have no usable role, breaking backend authorization and onboarding.
findings.trigger: Register or restore a student/parent account through the mobile adapter.
findings.evidence: Mobile calls Cognito signUp directly, reads custom:roles plural, and filters singular roles; backend writes custom:role and normally resolves plural Cognito groups, with role-specific clients.
findings.recommended_fix: Make backend registration/verification authoritative or reproduce it atomically in Cognito triggers, and publish one role/client normalization contract.

findings.title: Full Python suite is red, time-dependent, and leaks into real AWS clients
findings.impact: The suite cannot serve as a deployment or refactoring safety net and may contact ambient AWS configuration.
findings.trigger: Run full pytest on Python 3.12 or 3.14 at the audit date.
findings.evidence: Both environments reproducibly report 12 failed and 640 passed. Eleven failures stem from missing repository patches and stale dates; one stems from an unmocked ledger side effect.
findings.recommended_fix: Deny ambient AWS/network in tests, inject/freeze time, repair fixture placement and side-effect contracts without weakening assertions, and gate CI on target Python.

findings.title: JWT validation does not bind access tokens to allowed app clients and JWKS cannot rotate
findings.impact: Tokens from unintended clients in the pool may be accepted and signing-key rotation can cause persistent authentication failure until cold start.
findings.trigger: Use an access token from another app client in the same pool or a token signed by a newly rotated key.
findings.evidence: Audience verification is disabled, client_id is not checked, cache is global/unkeyed with no unknown-kid refresh, and synchronous JWKS HTTP runs in an async dependency.
findings.recommended_fix: Validate an explicit allowed client_id set, cache by issuer with TTL and refresh-on-unknown-key, use async/cold-start retrieval, and move group repair out of authentication.

findings.title: Upload size, type, ownership, and error controls are incomplete
findings.impact: Oversized or mislabeled objects can be stored/processed and internal S3 errors can be disclosed; ownership failure compounds SEC-003.
findings.trigger: Use a presigned PUT with large/mislabeled content or induce S3 presign failure.
findings.evidence: _MAX_FILE_SIZE is unused, PUT has no length policy, image/* is broad, no post-upload validation exists, and raw exception text is returned.
findings.recommended_fix: Use constrained presigned POST or equivalent, verify metadata and magic bytes before consumption, quarantine/scan, expire unused uploads, and redact errors.

findings.title: Student and model content can enter application logs
findings.impact: Minors' educational content and provider details may be retained in operational logs with broader access.
findings.trigger: Prompt injection match, malformed model JSON, or provider error.
findings.evidence: Logs include the first 120 characters of student input, 200 characters of model output, and full provider exception strings.
findings.recommended_fix: Log only structured event metadata, lengths, categories, exception class, and correlation IDs through centralized redaction.
required_tests: Captured logs exclude seeded student content

findings.title: Locked Python dependencies contain eight published advisories
findings.impact: Reachable framework, parsing, availability, or cryptographic weaknesses may remain in production dependencies.
findings.trigger: Run pip-audit against the locked requirements at the audit timestamp.
findings.evidence: pip-audit reports eight advisories across cryptography 48.0.0, ecdsa 0.19.2, pydantic-settings 2.14.1, python-multipart 0.0.29, and starlette 1.1.0. Reachability varies and was documented in the Markdown report.
findings.recommended_fix: Upgrade packages with fixes, remove unused vulnerable dependencies where possible, document temporary applicability exceptions with expiry, and gate new high/critical advisories.

findings.title: Checkout return URL validation accepts arbitrary HTTPS and localhost-prefix lookalikes
findings.impact: Checkout completion can redirect users to phishing or untrusted destinations.
findings.trigger: An authenticated parent supplies any HTTPS URL or a value such as http://localhost.evil.example.
findings.evidence: Validation uses startswith for https:// or http://localhost rather than parsed exact origins.
findings.recommended_fix: Parse structurally and allow only configured exact origins and paths per environment; do not accept caller-selected production origins.

findings.title: Practice mistake records discard the student's submitted answer
findings.impact: Mistake review always shows an empty user answer and loses learning-analysis context.
findings.trigger: Submit any incorrect practice answer and retrieve /practice/mistakes.
findings.evidence: The route reads student_answer but record_attempt neither accepts nor writes it; the response defaults missing student_answer to an empty string.
findings.recommended_fix: Persist a bounded normalized/display-safe student answer with schema version and treat legacy absence as unknown.

findings.title: Mobile request field names do not match backend Pydantic aliases
findings.impact: Mobile question submissions lose idempotency and push-token registrations lose device identity while appearing successful.
findings.trigger: Send the current mobile request payloads to FastAPI.
findings.evidence: Mobile sends idempotency_key and device_id; backend accepts idempotencyKey and deviceId. A direct Pydantic probe ignores snake-case idempotency_key.
findings.recommended_fix: Generate/share an OpenAPI client, use one casing convention, reject unexpected write fields, and validate every mobile payload against backend schemas.
required_tests: Push token device metadata round trip

findings.title: WebSocket service has no live route integration or mobile consumer
findings.impact: The advertised real-time notification path cannot operate end to end.
findings.trigger: Attempt a deployed connect/subscribe/fanout flow or search for route handlers/mobile client.
findings.evidence: Connection/fanout functions and tests exist, but no API Gateway route handler/IaC or mobile WebSocket consumer exists; live flags default false.
findings.recommended_fix: Complete route handlers, authorizer, deployment, mobile reconnect/dedupe, and live smoke, or explicitly scope v9 to polling.
findings.release_scope: Blocks any claimed real-time notification release.

findings.title: Login-code flow always reports deferred instead of authenticating
findings.impact: Users cannot complete a passwordless login flow despite its API/planning presence.
findings.trigger: Call the login-code endpoint under any supported account state.
findings.evidence: The route returns deferred and no tokens; existing tests assert that placeholder behavior.
findings.recommended_fix: Implement Cognito custom auth/OTP with delivery, expiry, anti-replay and anti-enumeration, or remove the endpoint and completion claim from the current scope.

findings.title: DynamoDB reads use scans, first-page filtering, and hard limits
findings.impact: Records can be silently omitted after page limits and read cost/latency grows with shared partitions.
findings.trigger: Datasets exceed the first DynamoDB page or 500 WebSocket connections.
findings.evidence: Queries filter broad partitions in Python without LastEvaluatedKey handling; challenge lookup uses a broad filtered query; fanout scans a hard-limited first page. No production benchmark was run.
findings.recommended_fix: Create access-pattern-specific keys/GSIs, use exact challenge keys, paginate all reads, and index recipient/channel connection lookup.

findings.title: Local runtime and Lambda artifact provenance drift from the target
findings.impact: Developers can validate a different runtime or inspect/deploy a stale package, and native behavior is not continuously proven.
findings.trigger: Use the checked-in .venv or verify the existing ignored dist artifact.
findings.evidence: Local Python is 3.14.5 versus target 3.12; dist verify reports a stale source hash. A fresh 3.12 manylinux arm64 build succeeds, isolating the problem to environment/artifact discipline.
findings.recommended_fix: Pin Python, create environments from lock, build once in CI, attest the manifest/digest, and promote that exact artifact through environments.

findings.title: Infrastructure, schema, and recovery are not reproducible from the repository
findings.impact: New environments, disaster recovery, index changes, and configuration review depend on undocumented external state.
findings.trigger: Attempt to bootstrap or restore an environment from this checkout.
findings.evidence: No IaC stack, DynamoDB bootstrap/migration, Cognito setup, WebSocket routes, bucket/queue definitions, backup, or restore implementation exists in the repository.
findings.recommended_fix: Version authoritative infrastructure and schema contracts, import existing resources, add additive checkpointed migrations, and automate sampled restore.

findings.title: Health, request correlation, metrics, and dependency readiness are incomplete
findings.impact: Operators cannot distinguish process liveness from dependency failure or correlate requests across Lambda and provider events.
findings.trigger: DynamoDB, S3, Cognito, queue, or provider degradation while /health remains static ok.
findings.evidence: Health is a constant response with version 0.1.0; no global request/trace middleware, consistent structured logs, application metrics, or repository-owned alarms are present.
findings.recommended_fix: Add liveness/readiness separation, correlation IDs, structured redacted logs, explicit timeouts, metrics, alarms, and runbooks.

findings.title: Planning completion materially overstates live product completion
findings.impact: Roadmap decisions can be based on local contract completion rather than integrated, live-verified user journeys.
findings.trigger: Treat v8.x completed milestones as product/live completion.
findings.evidence: Planning explicitly calls v8.4 a local gated operations contract and blocks real rollout, while mobile, WebSocket, providers, and production evidence remain incomplete. There are 1,835 planning files versus 83 backend source files.
findings.recommended_fix: Use separate contract-complete, integrated, live-verified, and product-complete states with executable evidence tied to SHA/environment.

findings.title: Oversized modules mix routing, policy, persistence, and provider concerns
findings.impact: Changes have large blast radius, failure injection is difficult, and authorization/business rules are more likely to diverge.
findings.trigger: Modify a cross-cutting flow in the listed modules.
findings.evidence: Large files combine policy, persistence, provider calls, and response construction; the source contains 46 broad except Exception sites.
findings.recommended_fix: After the correctness baseline, incrementally extract tested use-case and provider/repository boundaries without changing public APIs.

findings.title: Mobile tests validate strings rather than runtime behavior
findings.impact: Tests pass while the dependency tree is unresolvable, UI is placeholder-only, and request/auth contracts are incompatible.
findings.trigger: Run npm run test:contracts.
findings.evidence: The script checks dependency names, route-group substrings, and environment-variable substrings without importing, rendering, typechecking, calling an API, or building native code.
findings.recommended_fix: Retain cheap manifest checks but add typecheck, component, OpenAPI adapter, navigation, native build, and device E2E layers.

findings.title: Parent/student forward and reverse bindings can diverge
findings.impact: Authorization, parent dashboards, and deletion/reconciliation can disagree about the relationship.
findings.trigger: Timeout or exception after one binding put and before the reverse put/profile write.
findings.evidence: Forward CHILD and reverse PARENT records are independent put_item calls with no transaction.
findings.recommended_fix: Use TransactWriteItems with idempotent conditions and provide a historical symmetry reconciliation job.

findings.title: Rejected rate-limit attempts continue increasing counters
findings.impact: Repeated rejected retries inflate usage records and downstream failures consume quota without a result.
findings.trigger: Retry chat/hint after reaching the limit or after a downstream provider failure.
findings.evidence: The counter increments unconditionally and raises only when the returned count exceeds the limit.
findings.recommended_fix: Use a conditional capped increment and define accepted-versus-successful consumption semantics with operation idempotency.

findings.title: README, environment template, and codebase maps are stale
findings.impact: Developers receive incomplete setup/configuration guidance and stakeholders receive stale completion signals.
findings.trigger: Set up a clean environment or use planning maps to assess implementation.
findings.evidence: README is minimal, .env.example omits many active settings, maps do not reflect 43 test files, and mobile text claims unrendered controls.
findings.recommended_fix: Regenerate factual setup, configuration, module, and evidence documentation after runtime and IaC decisions stabilize.

findings.title: Lint and typing have no enforced baseline
findings.impact: New type and lint regressions cannot be separated from existing debt, and interface mismatches remain hidden.
findings.trigger: Run Ruff and mypy on the audit baseline.
findings.evidence: Ruff reports 5 errors; mypy reports 136 errors in 48 of 83 files; neither command is enforced in CI.
findings.recommended_fix: Fix Ruff, classify mypy errors, add required stubs, enforce a non-increasing baseline, and expand strict modules without Any-based suppression.
